metadata.career_relevance: DNF partition is the key to quicksort performance on duplicate-heavy data (common in Zerodha's trade prices). It also appears as 'Sort Colors' (LeetCode #75) in nearly every DSA screening.

hook.real_world_problem:
```
Zerodha trade status array:
['PENDING','EXECUTED','FAILED','EXECUTED','PENDING','FAILED']
Sort into: all PENDING | all EXECUTED | all FAILED

Brute force: sort → O(n log n)
Dutch National Flag: O(n) time, O(1) space — one pass!

Algorithm:
  low=0, mid=0, high=n-1
  while mid <= high:
    if arr[mid] == PENDING: swap(low, mid), low++, mid++
    if arr[mid] == EXECUTED: mid++
    if arr[mid] == FAILED: swap(mid, high), high--
```
hook.what_you_will_build: DNF partition for Sort Colors and custom 3-way categorization.

visual_aid.description: DNF array partitioned into 4 regions: [0..low-1] all 0s (red), [low..mid-1] all 1s (white), [mid..high] unknown (to process), [high+1..n-1] all 2s (blue). Arrows: low and mid advance together on 0, only mid advances on 1, high recedes on 2.

worked_example.problem_statement: Single pass sort of array containing only 0, 1, 2.

step_by_step_solution.details: low: next position for 0. mid: current element being processed. high: next position for 2. Everything between low and mid-1 is 1. When mid swaps with high: check swapped element (don't advance mid).

worked_example.final_code: int low=0,mid=0,high=n-1; while(mid<=high){if(nums[mid]==0) swap(low++,mid++); else if(nums[mid]==1) mid++; else swap(mid,high--);}
things_to_notice: When swapping mid with high: don't advance mid — need to re-examine the swapped element
Three invariants maintained throughout: left region=0, middle=1, right=2

industry_examples.use_case: Partition 1M orders into PENDING/EXECUTED/FAILED in O(n) O(1) space.

applications.interview_relevance: LeetCode #75 Sort Colors — asked in 20%+ of first DSA rounds at Flipkart, Amazon India. Key follow-up: 'Can you do it in one pass without extra space?' → DNF.
applications.real_world_workflow: 3-way partition: identify your 3 categories, map to 0/1/2, apply DNF. Also used for 3-way quicksort (faster on duplicate-heavy data like sorted or all-same arrays).

common_gaps.what_students_get_wrong: Incrementing mid after swapping with high. The element swapped from high hasn't been examined yet — it could be 0, 1, or 2.
common_gaps.remediation: Only increment mid when nums[mid] is 0 or 1 (element confirmed in correct region). When swapping with high, only decrement high — don't advance mid.
common_gaps.detection_pattern: Student writes: else { swap(mid, high); mid++; high--; } — incorrect, misses elements

key_takeaways: 3 pointers: low (next 0 position), mid (current), high (next 2 position)
Case 0: swap(low,mid), low++, mid++ — both advance
Case 1: mid++ only — element in place
Case 2: swap(mid,high), high-- only — mid stays (re-examine swapped)
One pass O(n), O(1) space — optimal for 3-value sort

self_check_questions.question: Why don't we increment mid after swapping nums[mid] with nums[high]?
self_check_questions.answer: When we swap nums[mid] with nums[high], the element that moves to position mid came from nums[high] — and we haven't examined it yet. It could be 0, 1, or 2. We must check it in the next iteration. Decrementing high is safe (that position is now confirmed as 2), but mid stays because it now contains an unexamined element.

reflection.next_topic_preview: Next: Array Synthesis — combine two pointers, sliding window, prefix sums, and DNF on harder problems.
